sections.markdown:
- 8 km easy aerobic run
- Keep this fully conversational after Tuesday's hill session.
- Flatten the effort on climbs and do not let the back half drift toward steady running.
- The job here is absorption and support, not reclaiming any quality volume trimmed from Tuesday.
- If Tuesday costs more than expected, shorten to `6 to 7 km`.

- This did not stay as the planned Wednesday, `2026-04-15`, `8 km` aerobic-support run. It also functionally replaced the missed Tuesday, `2026-04-14`, `12 km` hill session. The actual run covered `21.27 km` in `2:09:47` moving time (`2:13:44` elapsed) with about `220.2 m` of climbing, average HR `155.1`, and max HR `173`, so it landed `13.27 km` over Wednesday's plan and `1.27 km` over the combined Tuesday-plus-Wednesday planned volume.
- The stream data supports your read that you kept it controlled rather than raced it. The first `16.00 km` averaged about `6:07 /km` with average HR about `152.9` and about `114.0 m` of climbing, while the final `5.27 km` slowed to about `6:31 /km` as climbing rose to about `106.2 m` and average HR climbed to about `160.3`. That is the profile of a long aerobic run that got costlier late, not a disguised threshold session or an all-out half marathon.

- The immediate job is to absorb this as the week's long stimulus rather than stacking more work on top. With Monday's `7.124 km` already banked, the week sits at `28.394 km` after only two runs, and this single session already exceeded the planned Sunday long run by `6.27 km`.
- The rest of the week now needs to protect the ankles. Thursday drops to a short recovery check, Friday stays very light, Saturday defaults toward rest with basketball effectively off unless the ankles are clearly calm, and Sunday's planned long run becomes only a capped easy run rather than a second long effort.

- This still helps the current half-marathon and marathon build because it shows you can carry continuous `21 km` volume at a controlled average HR instead of turning every big day into a race. Used correctly, it can stand in as a useful durability and aerobic-support stimulus inside a consolidation week.
- The longer-term risk is not missing one hill session. It is following a controlled half marathon and lingering ankle load with enough extra mileage or court load to turn a useful aerobic day into a tissue-management problem. The durable move is to let this run count, keep the rest of the week cheap, and come back to sharper quality only after the ankles settle.
- Definitely added some load to both my ankles, but I tried to keep the full run as light as possible.
